## [06:04 - 09:05]

And this is something you usually want to set up right at the beginning is the automation rules within a Webber. So let's say you're setting up a buyer list. So the people that are going to be on this list will have purchased whatever product you have. And you want to unsubscribe them from your prospect list because you don't want to keep sending them mail if they've already purchased this particular product. And so this is where automation rules come into play. And so you can go and you can set up an action. So you can put unsubscribe people from prospect list when they subscribe to another list. So when people go and buy something on another list, you would unsubscribe them from this prospect list and you could set up the rule right here. Or maybe you want to add them to two lists. So when they subscribe to the prospect list, you also want to subscribe them to some master list that you have. So these automation rules are absolutely golden and they will help you to really segment your lists and diminish any confusion people will have, especially if they become a buyer, you don't want to keep sending them the same offer and keeping them on a prospect list. So these automation rules will help you to really organize your list building efforts. So very, very important. You definitely want to check that out. It's under automation.
